channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm Release Drops Rust Infostealer During Install
item.description:
Version&nbsp;8.14.0&nbsp;of the jscrambler npm package shipped with a malicious&nbsp;preinstall&nbsp;hook that silently drops and runs a native infostealer during installation, one build each for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Published on July 11, 2026, it needs no import and no CLI call. Installing 8.14.0 is enough to run&nbsp;it.
Socket flagged the release&nbsp;six minutes after it was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of sustained cyber espionage activity against several Pakistani law enforcement organizations undertaken by suspected China- and India-aligned threat actors between February 2024 and April 2026.
"At Balochistan Police, the compromised assets included servers hosting web applications that manage police and citizen data, such as criminal and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign
item.description: <p>Multiple campaigns are using ghost accounts to map GitHub organizations, including their repositories and members.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ghost-accounts-abuse-github-api-in-mass-recon-campaign/">Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Critical Zimbra Flaw Could Let Crafted Emails Run Malicious Code in User Sessions
item.description:
Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution.
The vulnerability has been described as a case of stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that could allow specially crafted emails to execute malicious scripts in a user's session. It has yet to be assigned a CVE identifier.
"The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals 25,000 Compromised WordPress Websites
item.description: <p>An exposed WP-SHELLSTORM server revealed tools, logs, cloud credentials, and thousands of webshells used in a large website hacking campaign.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-wp-shellstorm-wordpress-webshell-attack/">Exposed Server Reveals 25,000 Compromised WordPress Websites</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: “Squidbleed” Vulnerability
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Jen Ellis: Connecting Cyber Community With Political Machinery
item.description: Security Pro File: On the heels of her recent honors as a Member of the Order of the British Empire (MBE), we take a look back at the events that shaped Jen Ellis' advocacy on behalf of security researchers.
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski

item.title: Claude Code Espionage Campaign Exposes a New Enterprise AI Risk
item.description: <p>Anthropic’s AI-run espionage report shows why enterprises need stronger governance for AI agents, MCP connectors, and enterprise data access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-anthropic-claude-code-ai-agent-governance-risk/">Claude Code Espionage Campaign Exposes a New Enterprise AI Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Massive Telstra Outage Hits Mobile Networks, Rail, and Payments in Australia
item.description: <p>A Telstra software defect disrupted mobile services, rail communications, payments, and emergency calls across Australia, exposing infrastructure risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-telstra-software-defect-network-outage-australia-apac/">Massive Telstra Outage Hits Mobile Networks, Rail, and Payments in Australia</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Cybercriminals Flock to Healthcare Businesses as Attacks Surge
item.description: While cyberattacks against hospitals and clinics grew modestly in the first half of 2026, attacks on service providers and other healthcare businesses more than doubled.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description:
Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external security threat."
The company has temporarily disabled access to the affected accounts, a step it says it took "out of an abundance of caution" while it works with internal and external security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description:
Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases.
The compromised version, @injectivelabs/sdk-ts@1.20.21, came embedded with fake telemetry functionality that exfiltrated data from cryptocurrency wallets. The version was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Details Incident Response to Exposed AWS GovCloud Keys
item.description: CISA reveals how it responded after sensitive AWS GovCloud credentials and internal data were exposed in a public GitHub repository

item.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description:
Researchers at firmware security firm&nbsp;Binarly&nbsp;have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center servers.
Four of the bugs can crash a device. The other two could let an attacker who slips a malicious image in front of the bootloader run their own code, before the device
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New York Bans Smart Glasses Across 1,240 Courts
item.description: <p>New York will ban Meta and other smart glasses from all 1,240 state courts starting July 20 over privacy and recording concerns.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-new-york-smart-glasses-court-ban/">New York Bans Smart Glasses Across 1,240 Courts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Microsoft Warns New 'GigaWiper' Malware Combines Espionage and Destructive Capabilities
item.description: A new multi-purpose backdoor allows cyber threat actors to conduct both quiet espionage activity and destructive wiping operations

item.title: In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops
item.description: <p>Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Abnormal AI sued by Anthropic, AssuranceAmerica data breach affects 7 million people, NSA brings back TAO.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/in-other-news-dhs-database-hacked-adobe-boosts-patch-cadence-canada-disrupts-ransomware-ops/">In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
item.description:
Researchers at Ledger's&nbsp;Donjon security team&nbsp;have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the attacker picks.
No old password. No backup card. Once it is reset, whoever did it controls the wallet and can move the coins&nbsp;out.
This is not an emergency for most owners. The attack needs
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
item.description:
Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution on the host.
A brief description of the high-severity vulnerabilities is as follows&nbsp;-
GHSA-hjr6-g723-hmfm (CVSS score: 8.8) - An operating system
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from July 6–10.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-breakthroughs-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week/">AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Fresh ATM Crypto Software Bugs: Jackpot or Bust?
item.description: Organizations, and possibly ATMs, are at risk of compromise, thanks to holes in a Microsoft BitLocker security wrapper.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: More Countries Jump on the Social Media 'Ban Wagon'
item.description: Age restrictions on accounts may be more of a stopgap because industry compliance is already falling short. Tech giants are struggling to follow the laws without affecting users.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Anthropic and OpenAI Security Tools Could Fuel Cyber-Attacks, Researchers Warn
item.description: Researchers at the AI Now Institute developed a proof-of-concept exploit showing common AI tools used for security could backfire

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring PROSA
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
item.description:
The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON.
Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the threat cluster may appear like a low-sophistication, high-activity operation that propagates malware via counterfeit installers using SEO poisoning techniques, it belies their true organizational
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
item.description: AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: New Ransomware Exploits Malicious Driver to Remove Cybersecurity Protections
item.description: GodDamn ransomware uses remote desktop application to secretly move around networks and drop the malicious PoisonX kernel driver

item.title: Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description: <p>Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, was sentenced to 70 months for helping the BlackCat/Alphv group.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/third-us-security-expert-sentenced-to-prison-for-helping-ransomware-gang/">Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Best Tools for Securing MCP and LLM Integrations | UpGuard
item.description: Secure your AI infrastructure. Learn how to protect shadow MCP and LLM integrations using the best tools for attack surface discovery and runtime defenses.

item.title: China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force
item.description: <p>Both foes and allies have targeted the Balochistan Police force in Pakistan for at least two years, according to SentinelOne.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/china-india-linked-hackers-both-targeted-same-pakistani-police-force/">China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description:
A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch.
FoxIO researcher Sébastien Féry&nbsp;disclosed the flaw on July 8&nbsp;and nicknamed it XRING. He says it needs no login and no malformed packets: about 260 bytes of ordinary QPACK traffic takes the server
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description:
Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability Report, only 45% of organizations consolidate their asset and exposure data into a single view, and every downstream security program inherits whatever the inventory gets wrong.
Lumen Technologies, a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Raises Bio Bounty to $50,000 for Universal Jailbreaks
item.description: <p>OpenAI has doubled its top bio bounty to $50,000 for researchers who can develop a universal jailbreak against its biological safety challenge. The ongoing private program begins with GPT-5.6 and keeps GPT-5.5 in scope through July 27, 2026.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-bio-bounty-jailbreak/">OpenAI Raises Bio Bounty to $50,000 for Universal Jailbreaks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description:
A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists naming more than 1.4 million websites.
Far fewer were actually broken into, but the exposed files showed researchers how a mass site-hacking operation runs from the inside.
The operation, now tracked as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers
item.description: <p>The attackers call victims to direct them to phishing websites mirroring Microsoft Entra ID login pages.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/okta-warns-of-vishing-attacks-targeting-microsoft-365-customers/">Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: AI Surveillance and Social Progress
item.description: <p>In the near future, <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/artificialintelligenceai">AI</a>-powered surveillance systems will be able to track everything we do in public, and much of what we do in private. And if we do something wrong&#8212;shoplift, litter, jaywalk, you name it&#8212;the system will notice, retain it, tie it to your official government record, communicate that fact to you, and provide real-time alerts to any relevant authorities&#8230; and maybe also to the general public.</p> <p>Think of these systems as automated speed cameras, but on steroids. Only they&#8217;ll enforce not just speed limits, but any other rule you can imagine. And you won&#8217;t receive a ticket weeks later by mail; you&#8217;ll be informed about and fined for your violation immediately...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Study of 281 Free Android VPN Apps Finds Traffic Leaks, Unencrypted Data, and Tracking
item.description:
Researchers ran 281 of the most popular free VPN apps on the Google Play Store through a new testing system and found that many fail at the basics people install a VPN for, i.e., keeping their traffic private and secure.
The apps flagged with at least one problem have been installed more than 2.4 billion times.
The problems are basic, not sophisticated. 29 apps let user traffic leak outside
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
item.description:
A threat actor has been targeting organizations spanning multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that prompt Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey with an aim to carry out data extortion attacks.
The threat actor, tracked by Okta under the moniker O-UNC-066, has deployed a panel-controlled phishing kit that's capable of targeting the passkey enrollment process. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Increase in Number of Security Updates
item.description: Microsoft has said the volume of Windows security updates is set to grow as it uses AI to find new bugs

item.title: GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage
item.description: <p>The backdoor’s destructive capabilities include a standalone wiper, ransomware encryption, and a multi-pass wiping command.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/gigawiper-combines-multiple-malware-for-system-level-sabotage/">GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain Over $5 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description:
Security firm&nbsp;Coinspect&nbsp;has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls&nbsp;Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words that control the money. When that phrase is made with weak randomness, an attacker can work it out and take everything it controls.
The firm has confirmed one coordinated sweep on May 27
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: NHS Warns Staff Over Unauthorized Access to Patient Data
item.description: NHS tells staff they could face prison for “inappropriate” access to patients’ medical records

item.title: ‘HalluSquatting’ Turns AI Hallucinations Into Botnet Delivery Mechanism
item.description: <p>Researchers demonstrate adversarial hallucination squatting against popular AI assistants to achieve remote code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hallusquatting-turns-ai-hallucinations-into-botnet-delivery-mechanism/">&#8216;HalluSquatting&#8217; Turns AI Hallucinations Into Botnet Delivery Mechanism</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description:
A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operators to extort multiple victims and working with two other cybersecurity professionals to target additional victims in 2023.
In a sentencing memorandum, federal prosecutors described Martino as a "
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection
item.description: <p>A Go module is used to load PowerShell code that fetches a resolver from public dead drops to execute Windows malware.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/network-of-200-github-repositories-used-for-malware-infection/">Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Iran's Cyber Crosshairs Focus Beyond Critical Infrastructure
item.description: Obscurity isn't a defense. If your company has any Internet-facing vulnerability, you're at risk from multiple threats.
item.dc:creator: Joe Slowik

item.title: Microsoft Reins in RoguePlanet Zero-Day Threat
item.description: The researcher known as &quot;Nightmare-Eclipse&quot; published a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for the Windows Defender vulnerability in early June after dropping several other Microsoft zero-days.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: China Warns of Claude Code ‘Backdoor’ Security Risk
item.description: <p>China warned organizations to remove certain Claude Code versions over alleged backdoor risks, while Anthropic called the feature anti-abuse protection.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-china-claude-code-backdoor-security-risk-apac/">China Warns of Claude Code ‘Backdoor’ Security Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
item.description: If you're handling AI agents like a service account or API token, consider yourself behind. AI agents need a fundamentally different approach.
item.dc:creator: Mora Gozani

item.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description:
Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub&nbsp;API.
"Operators rely on automated scraping tooling with custom or legitimate-sounding user agents, leveraging GitHub 'ghost' accounts that are often years old, or compromised OAuth tokens and personal
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description:
Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands the operator can choose from.
Each is a different way to break a machine: wipe the whole disk, overwrite the Windows drive, or run fake "ransomware" that scrambles files with a key it never saves
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description:
GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA).
The Microsoft-owned subsidiary noted that the following npm install behaviors that used to run automatically before have been made opt-in&nbsp;-
allowScripts defaults to off, meaning
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description:
Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch&nbsp;it.
This week is full of that kind of damage. Not loud. Not clever. Just small gaps doing big jobs. The worst part is how normal it all looks until the bill arrives.
The full ThreatsDay list is below.
Global
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform
item.description: <p>The Israeli company has developed a cryptographic posture and post-quantum cryptography management platform.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/qiz-security-raises-17-million-for-cryptographic-governance-platform/">QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Game Plans
item.description: The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyber warfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge
item.description: <p>Two announcements on July 7, 2026, demonstrate the government’s determination to improve the level of cybersecurity within the UK.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/uk-government-rolls-out-agentic-ai-defense-plan-alongside-industry-pledge/">UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: AssuranceAmerica Data Breach: 6.9M Driver’s License Numbers Exposed
item.description: <p>AssuranceAmerica says hackers accessed the driver's license data of 6.9 million customers, exposing personal information and raising concerns about identity theft.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-assuranceamerica-data-breach-drivers-license-customers/">AssuranceAmerica Data Breach: 6.9M Driver’s License Numbers Exposed</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Discord Bug Wrongly Banned 8,000 Users Over Harmless Images
item.description: <p>Discord says a moderation bug wrongly banned more than 8,000 users after harmless images triggered its automated safety system.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-discord-moderation-bug-wrongful-bans/">Discord Bug Wrongly Banned 8,000 Users Over Harmless Images</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Vibe-Coded Malware Caught in Active Directory Attack
item.description: Huntress found a threat actor using vibe-coded PowerShell to map an Active Directory network

item.title: UK Cyber Resilience Pledge Sets 90-Day Test for Security Leaders
item.description: <p>The UK Cyber Resilience Pledge gives organizations a 30/60/90-day checklist for NCSC alerts, supplier Cyber Essentials checks and board-level cyber governance.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cyber-resilience-pledge-emea-uk/">UK Cyber Resilience Pledge Sets 90-Day Test for Security Leaders</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
item.description: A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description:
AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the next host before your team clears the first alert.
That is the gap, and it is not your fault. The tools and runbooks most teams run on were built for attackers who work at human speed. AI-driven
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 75% CISOs Fear Executives Don’t Understand Cybersecurity Risks Employees Face
item.description: Survey of cybersecurity leaders by MetaCompliance finds that many feel boards are uninterested in ever-evolving cyber risks

item.title: Chinese-Funded Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown Leads to 5,800 Arrests
item.description: Operation First Light 2026, coordinated by Interpol and funded by the Chinese government, has led to 5,811 arrests

item.title: The Language of AI Could Change How Humans Speak
item.description: <p>Because of the way they are trained, large language models capture only a slice of human language. They&#8217;re trained on the written word, from textbooks to social media posts, and our speech as captured in movies and on television. These models have minimal access to the unscripted conversations we have face to face or voice to voice. This is the vast majority of speech, and a vital component of human culture.</p> <p>There&#8217;s a risk to this. The increased use of large language models means we humans will encounter much more AI-generated text. We humans, in turn, will begin to adopt the linguistic patterns and behaviors of these models. This will affect not just how we communicate with one another, but also how we ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we announced it — built quietly months earlier, heads down, taking findings and shipping fixes, because customers kept asking us to. We only announced it now because everyone else started announcing theirs,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GhostApproval Flaw Hits Six Major AI Coding Assistants
item.description: Wiz discovered GhostApproval, a symlink flaw in six major AI coding assistants that bypasses approval

item.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According to a new report published by the Threat Hunter Team from Symantec, the ransomware was first publicly spotted in the wild on May 21, 2026. It's assessed to be a rebrand of the Beast ransomware,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies
item.description: Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: New AI Security Charter Backed by Over 70 Cyber Firms
item.description: Over 70 cybersecurity organizations have signed the CREST AI Charter detailing responsible use of AI for security

item.title: Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
item.description:
Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a privilege escalation issue in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine ("mpengine.dll"), which provides scanning, detection, and cleaning capabilities for its antivirus and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: European Organizations Have a Collaboration Security Confidence Gap
item.description: A new survey shows security leaders have an inflated sense of safety regarding their collaboration tools and platforms.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Cybercriminals Plant Malicious AI Agents in Open Source Tool Repositories
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers at ESET identify big rise in suspicious and malicious toolsets which put users at risk from cyber-attacks

item.title: Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description:
Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default.
"You can also @-mention Instagram accounts in the Meta AI app to bring specific Instagram profiles right into your images," the social media giant said in a post.
"Whether you want to design a custom event invitation
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a&nbsp;proof-of-concept published Wednesday&nbsp;by the AI Now Institute, an attack it calls "Friendly Fire." It works against Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex when either is running in an autonomous mode that approves its own
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description:
Researchers at&nbsp;Wiz&nbsp;found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one harmless-looking file, but the write lands on a sensitive one instead.
The affected tools are Amazon Q Developer, Anthropic's Claude Code, Augment, Cursor, Google Antigravity, and Windsurf.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230 lookalike domains.
The activity dates back to at least August 2022, according to DNS threat intelligence firm Infoblox. Once such campaign, observed earlier this year, involved the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Cyber Threat Detection Tools & Software | UpGuard
item.description: The best cyber threat detection tools and software in 2026. Learn how to protect your endpoints, logs, and external digital surfaces from a breach.

item.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
item.description: The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
item.description: The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Windows 11 Cleanup Guide: 9 Ways to Make Your PC Feel Faster
item.description: <p>Make Windows 11 feel faster with nine cleanup tips for startup apps, storage, background processes, updates, malware scans, and more.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-11-cleanup-guide/">Windows 11 Cleanup Guide: 9 Ways to Make Your PC Feel Faster</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matt Gonzales

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description:
Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders.
The agents are not malicious. They just do a lot of things that, to a behavioral engine, look exactly like an attack.
Decrypting browser credentials, listing what sits in Windows' credential store,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Job Offers Impersonate Netflix, OpenAI, and FIFA to Steal Google Credentials
item.description: <p>A fake recruitment phishing campaign impersonates major brands and uses trusted HR platforms to steal Google account credentials.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-recruitment-phishing-google-credentials-2026/">Fake Job Offers Impersonate Netflix, OpenAI, and FIFA to Steal Google Credentials</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Vidar Infostealer Hammers SMBs via Malvertising Campaign
item.description: A financially motivated operation uses lures of cracked or pirated software to deliver a two-for-one malware combo for data theft and cryptomining.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Australia’s Teen Social Media Ban Exposes Age Verification Gap
item.description: <p>Australia’s under-16 social media ban faces scrutiny after testers found major platforms did not ask for age proof.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-australia-teen-social-media-ban-age-checks-apac/">Australia’s Teen Social Media Ban Exposes Age Verification Gap</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Hacker Claims Accenture Breach Exposed Source Code, SSH Keys, and Azure Tokens
item.description: <p>Accenture confirmed a breach after a hacker claimed 35GB of source code and cloud keys were stolen, raising questions for cloud and security teams.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-accenture-breach-cloud-keys/">Hacker Claims Accenture Breach Exposed Source Code, SSH Keys, and Azure Tokens</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: RedWing Android Spyware Sold as a Service on Telegram
item.description: Zimperium found RedWing, an Android spyware sold as a service via Telegram to target banking apps

item.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
item.description:
AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist.
New research, which its authors call&nbsp;HalluSquatting, turns that habit into an attack: work out the fake names an AI reliably invents, register them first, and wait for the assistant to fetch your trap on a user's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ubiquiti Patches Critical UniFi Flaws Across Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS
item.description:
Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation and arbitrary command execution.
The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2026-50746 (CVSS score: 10.0) - An improper access control vulnerability in UniFi Connect Application that an attacker
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked APT Expands Proxy Network With New Malware
item.description: Cisco Talos said China-linked APT UAT-7810 is growing its proxy relay network with new malware

item.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
item.description:
A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. This “ghost phishing” technique keeps the malicious page hidden until it decrypts and comes to life inside the victim’s browser.
For security leaders, the risk is clear: traditional URL checks may miss the attack while Microsoft 365 access, sensitive data, and response time
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SCMBANKER Malware Uses ClickFix Lures to Target Mexican Banking Users
item.description:
A new banking fraudulent operation is targeting customers of Mexican banks, fintech, payment processors, and cryptocurrency exchanges using ClickFix lures.
The activity cluster, tracked by Elastic Security Labs under the moniker REF6045, involves infecting victims through fake CAPTCHA verification pages that deceive them into running a malicious command that installs a PowerShell toolkit dubbed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Threat Actors Uses Agentic AI to Rapidly Compromise Cloud Target
item.description: Sygnia report details how agentic AI accelerated weeks-long attack to just 72 hours

item.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
item.description:
New research shows that a signed Git commit's hash is not the one-of-a-kind name that much of the software world assumes it to be. Given any signed commit, someone without the signing key can mint a second commit with the same files, author, and date, and a valid signature, GitHub still stamps "Verified."
Everything a reviewer would check matches. The commit's hash does not. That matters
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
item.description:
For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. Credential stuffing was cheap, scalable, and for defenders, relatively well understood.
That era is ending. Not because attackers gave up, but because the front door finally got harder to kick in.
Passkeys are now mainstream.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
item.description:
An AI coding assistant that refuses to answer a dangerous request in its chat box can answer it anyway if the same request is broken into small, ordinary-looking steps inside a code editor. That is the finding of a&nbsp;new study of GitHub Copilot&nbsp;by researchers Abhishek Kumar and Carsten Maple.
The models they tested through Copilot, Claude from Anthropic, and Gemini from Google, refused
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cybersecurity and the Gap Between Skill and Ability
item.description: <p>Last week, national security agencies from the Five Eyes&#8212;that&#8217;s the rich, English-language-speaking countries club&#8212;jointly released a <a href="https://www.nsa.gov/Press-Room/News-Highlights/Article/Article/4523810/five-eyes-cyber-security-agencies-statement/">statement</a> warning of the increasing cyber risks of AI models: in particular, their ability to autonomously hack into systems and networks. The statement was more measured than some of the <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/22/anthropic-claude-fable-ai-model-artificial-intelligence-national-security">breathless headlines</a> about it, and the advice they gave is pretty much the standard advice everyone gives&#8212;albeit with newfound urgency.</p> <p>Internet risks are nothing new, and cyberattacks&#8212;both large and small&#8212;have been a significant issue since long before the current crop of generative AI models...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Malicious Campaign Delivers Vidar Infostealer and Monero Crypto Miner
item.description: Cyber threat actors are infecting victims with the Vidar stealer and the XMRig cryptocurrency miner in a new malicious campaign

item.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
item.description:
A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices.
According to findings from Cisco Talos, UAT-7810 is an advanced persistent threat (APT) actor that's responsible for maintaining and proliferating LapDogs, an ORB network that first came to light in June 2025.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
